name: Oakland to San Jose LTL
hero-headline: Oakland, CA to San Jose, CA LTL Freight
subheadline: 45 miles. 1-2 business day transit. 4 carriers active on this lane.
body-content:
Lane Overview: Oakland to San Jose

The Oakland to San Jose LTL lane covers approximately 45 miles between the West Coast and West Coast regions. Standard transit runs 1-2 business days depending on carrier routing and terminal schedules. Common equipment on this corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Cross-dock facilities at Bay Area Hub, LA Gateway, Inland Empire support consolidation and transit optimization. Peak volumes Aug-Oct (produce season). Rate pressure Dec-Jan.

How WARP Operates the Oakland to San Jose Lane

WARP covers the Oakland to San Jose corridor with 4 vetted carriers providing LTL capacity.

  • Service capabilities on this lane include: Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery; Estimated 1-2 business day transit window; Pallet-level tracking with delivery appointment scheduling.
  • Shipments receive pallet-level tracking from pickup through delivery with appointment scheduling and delivery confirmation.
  • Nearby cross-dock operations at Bay Area Hub and LA Gateway enable consolidation and re-routing when exceptions occur.
  • Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change.
  • WARP's operations team manages carrier coordination, rescheduling, and resolution without requiring shipper follow-up.

Operating Details

Key operational considerations for this lane:

  • Estimated lane distance: 45 miles (Oakland to San Jose)
  • Transit window: 1-2 business days for standard LTL service
  • Available equipment: Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled)
  • Active carriers on this lane: 4
  • Nearest cross-dock facilities: Bay Area Hub, LA Gateway, Inland Empire
  • Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery milestones
  • Exception alerts within 30 minutes of status changes with automated escalation
  • Delivery appointment scheduling and confirmation managed by WARP operations

Pricing & Rate Factors

Estimated LTL rates on the Oakland to San Jose lane range from $250 to $300, depending on freight class, weight, pallet count, and seasonal demand.

  • Key cost drivers include NMFC freight classification, shipment density, and whether accessorials such as liftgate, inside delivery, or limited-access pickup apply.
  • At 45 miles, the linehaul distance is the largest cost component (approximately $6.
  • 11/mile).
  • Fuel surcharges are applied as a separate line item based on current diesel prices.
  • These are modeled estimates.

Enter your shipment details to get a real-time, all-in rate.

Validate This Lane

Validate this lane with a controlled pilot: Oakland to San Jose LTL (45-mile corridor; 1–2 day transit window; 4 vetted carriers active; cross-dock access at Bay Area Hub and LA Gateway). Track quote response time, transit predictability, and exception trends before scaling volume. Start with a single shipment to benchmark WARP against your current process.

Related Freight Pages

Metro hubs: Oakland Freight Hub · San Jose Freight Hub

faq-schema:
How much does LTL freight from Oakland, CA to San Jose, CA cost?

Estimated LTL rates range from $250 to $300 based on freight class, weight, and pallet count. Actual rates depend on shipment specifics and current market conditions. Get an instant quote for real-time pricing.

What is the transit time for LTL shipments from Oakland to San Jose?

Standard LTL transit on this 45-mile lane is 1-2 business days. Actual transit depends on carrier routing, terminal schedules, and pickup timing.

What carriers does WARP use on the Oakland to San Jose lane?

WARP maintains a vetted network of approximately 4 active carriers on this corridor. Carrier selection is optimized for each shipment based on equipment requirements, transit targets, and performance history.

Can I track my LTL shipment from Oakland to San Jose in real time?

Yes. Every shipment includes real-time tracking with scan events at pickup, in-transit checkpoints, and delivery. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change, and WARP's operations team manages resolution directly.

What equipment is available for LTL freight on this lane?

Common equipment on the Oakland to San Jose corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Equipment availability varies by season and demand. Specify your requirements when requesting a quote.
